entry.title: Professional Services in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your 2026 updates for the Professional Services industry, here you can learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 in the area of Professional Services. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Intercompany cost allocations reconciliation review booklet (16T, 4AN) 
  • View custom fields in the billing elements list of the Manage Project Billing app (4E9) 
  • Enablement of time and expense billing without open billing plan item (4E9) 
  • Archival of Project Billing data (4E9) 
  • Seamless access to Preliminary Billing Document apps on creation of Project Down Payment Request (4E9) 
  • Improving usability for projects managers to adjust internal projects within professional service projects (J11)
  • Project staffing view for Resource Managers (6JO)
  • Ability to find resources across resource organizations (6JO)
  • Custom content in Resource Management apps (6JO)

Here you can have a look at the three top highlights of this release for the professional services industry here:

Intercompany cost allocations reconciliation review booklet (16T, 4AN) 

Reconciling intercompany costs is a common challenge that can complicate period-end closing. To provide complete transparency, we have delivered the "Intercompany Cost Allocations Reconciliation" review booklet. This powerful report gives the delivering company a comprehensive, step-by-step view of the entire resource-related intercompany billing process. It bridges the gap between the initial cost (the CO journal entry) and the internal billing document, clearly highlighting any unbilled items. With the 2602 release, the report is further enhanced to trace the process all the way to the corresponding FI journal entry on the receiving company's books, allowing you to quickly identify and close any reconciliation gaps.

 

This was also a request raised by customers on our Customer Influence Portal, which was resolved with this delivery: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/285049

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

 

Please also have a look at Thorsten Seegers blog, he is the product manager experts of this topic.

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Next let's get to the topics we have this release in the project billing area.

Please remember that Andreas Hammerschmidt updates his detailed blog “Project Billing for Customer Projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud” with every release, so you can find a comprehensive overview of the functionality there.

 

View custom fields in the billing elements list of the Manage Project Billing app (4E9) 

To make truly informed billing decisions, you need the full context. We've enhanced the "Manage Project Billing" app to provide just that. Imagine your business uses custom fields on sales orders to track critical data like a "Project Priority Code," "Geographic Region," or a "Contractual Milestone ID." Now, you can add these fields as columns directly in your billing worklist. This simple change has a powerful impact: you can now sort your billing elements by priority to ensure high-value items are processed first, or filter by region to manage tax-specific requirements. This brings essential data to the forefront early in the process, reducing the need to cross-reference information and helping you prevent billing errors before they happen.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Enablement of time and expense billing without open billing plan item (4E9) 

Your ability to generate cash flow shouldn't be held up by administrative hurdles. To accelerate your time-to-bill, we've introduced a major flexibility enhancement for Time and Expense or Usage-Based billing. Consider a scenario where ad-hoc consulting work is completed and time is recorded, but a specific billing plan item for that exact period doesn't exist yet. Instead of waiting for a plan to be created, billing specialists can now immediately create a Billing Document Request for this work. The system intelligently handles the backend process by automatically generating a new billing plan item with a due date set to the current date. This ad-hoc billing capability, enabled via a feature toggle, ensures that you can invoice for completed work the moment it's ready, directly improving your cash flow.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Archival of Project Billing data (4E9) 

 Maintaining a lean, high-performing system is crucial for long-term operational excellence. To support this, we've expanded our data archiving capabilities. When a sales order tied to a customer project is closed and archived, the system now intelligently archives all associated Project Billing data along with it. This ensures that the complete historical record of a project is stored together in a secure, accessible archive. For system administrators, this means a reduced data footprint and better application performance. For compliance and auditing, this provides a clear and complete trail. An auditor-role user can easily view the count of archived entries, and the application log provides full transparency, confirming exactly which project billing requests were archived as part of the process.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Seamless access to Preliminary Billing Document apps on creation of Project Down Payment Request (4E9) 

A smooth workflow is a fast workflow. We've optimized the process for creating project down payment requests to eliminate unnecessary steps and keep you moving. Previously, after submitting a down payment request, the confirmation dialog would only link to the billing document app. This created a disjointed experience, forcing you to manually navigate to other apps to continue your work. With the 2602 release, the success dialog has been enhanced. It now includes direct navigation links to both the "Create Preliminary Billing Document" and "Manage Preliminary Billing Documents - Services" apps. This means you can flow from one logical task to the next without interruption, saving clicks and maintaining your focus.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Improving usability for projects managers to adjust internal projects within professional service projects (J11)

A great user experience is paramount. We've rolled out several enhancements to make project administration more intuitive and flexible. This includes new, streamlined Fiori apps like "Manage Projects – Professional Services Projects" for creating internal projects and "Manage Work Packages" for maintaining work-package demands. We've also listened to your feedback to provide more flexibility. You can now assign a resource to a project responsibility even if their work agreement starts in the future, allowing for better forward planning. Furthermore, the Project Control app now provides a complete financial picture by displaying all related Purchase Orders and Purchase Requisitions, giving you a single source of truth for all project commitments. This flexibility even extends to how you find information, as you can now edit certain SAP-delivered search models and publish them to your catalog, tailoring the search experience to your team's needs

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at these two entries for this topic on SAP Help Portal

 

Project staffing view for Resource Managers (6JO)

Effective resource management requires a bird's-eye view of project needs. The new "Manage Request Assignments" app delivers exactly that. It provides resource managers with a consolidated, project-centric dashboard showing all resource requests and their assignment status. Instead of digging into individual requests, you can now see the entire staffing picture for a project at a weekly or monthly level. This makes it easy to spot upcoming resource shortfalls or identify overstaffed areas at a glance. From this central view, you can directly navigate to the "Staff Resource Request" or "Manage Resource Utilization" apps to make proactive adjustments, ensuring optimal staffing across your entire project portfolio.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Ability to find resources across resource organizations (6JO)

In a global or matrixed organization, talent can be anywhere. Our latest update breaks down internal silos to help you find the best person for the job, regardless of their home department. Resource Managers can now use the new "Resource Organization" filter in the "Staff Resource Requests" app. This powerful filter allows you to expand your search and view matching resources from multiple resource organizations you are authorized to access. The results will clearly display the "Resource Organization" for each candidate, empowering you to build the best possible project team by tapping into a wider, more diverse internal talent pool.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

 

Custom content in Resource Management apps (6JO)

We understand that every business has unique data needs for making staffing decisions. To accommodate this, you can now add custom sections and content to Resource Management apps using our built-in UI extensibility tools. For example, you could add a custom section to the Resource Request page to display an employee's "Security Clearance Level," "Industry Certifications," or "Language Skills." This ensures that all critical decision-making information is available in one place. This level of customization is supported by our robust framework, which includes a library of released CDS views like `I_LglCntntMCntntTypeTxtAPI01` (Document Content Type - Text), giving your developers stable, SAP-approved objects to build upon.

 

More Information

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blog posts on Professional Services, please have a look at my collection blog  and also take a look at the Community page for Professional Services .

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

Find All of Our Assets   

We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Guia Prático para Solução de Problemas – Workflow Flexível
entry.content:
O uso do Workflow Flexível oferece inúmeras vantagens, especialmente por viabilizar uma solução robusta e standard para os processos de aprovação de documentos no sistema. No entanto, apesar de sua abrangência e flexibilidade, sua correta operação depende de um conjunto de configurações técnicas, organizacionais e funcionais. Dessa forma, é fundamental que os profissionais envolvidos compreendam os principais pontos de atenção para lidar com eventuais inconsistências ao longo do processo.

Existem diversas ferramentas disponíveis para identificação e correção de erros de workflow. A proposta deste material não é esgotar todas as possibilidades, mas apresentar aquelas mais utilizadas na prática, auxiliando na condução eficiente das análises.

Investigação do erro

O primeiro passo é identificar qual tipo de erro está ocorrendo para, então, seguir para a resolução. Para isso, existem algumas ferramentas que podem ser utilizadas

1. SWIA: é utilizada para monitorar e administrar as instâncias de workflow, exibindo o status das tarefas ao longo do processo. Normalmente, este é o primeiro ponto a ser consultado durante a investigação, pois permite identificar em qual etapa o fluxo se encontra e se há indícios iniciais de falha

Os status mais comuns das tarefas são:

  • Ready / Pronto: tarefa disponível na caixa de aprovação do aprovador;
  • Completed / Encerrado: a ação de aprovação foi executada pelo aprovador;
  • Error / Incorreto: quando algum problema impede que a tarefa de aprovação chegue até o aprovador.

Para acessar a mensagem de erro de uma tarefa com status Incorreto, seguimos o seguinte caminho:

Em muitos casos, a SWIA fornece informações preliminares relevantes. No entanto, quando as mensagens apresentadas não são suficientes para identificar a causa raiz, é necessário complementar a análise com outras ferramentas.

2. SWI2_DIAG: é utilizada para diagnosticar workflows que se encontram em status de erro. Por meio dela, é possível analisar logs técnicos detalhados, identificar falhas de processamento e, quando aplicável, reprocessar ou reiniciar o fluxo após a correção das inconsistências.

3. PFAC: permite a validação das regras de determinação de agentes. Nela, é possível simular se uma determinada regra funciona corretamente para um usuário específico, evitando a necessidade de reiniciar múltiplas vezes o workflow ou gerar documentos apenas para testes.

Essa funcionalidade é fundamental para diferenciar falhas técnicas de problemas relacionados à estrutura organizacional ou às regras de aprovação.

4. SWELS: inicia ou desliga o trace/rastreamento de eventos, pode ser ativado para um usuário específico;

5. SWEL: exibe o histórico de rastreamento de eventos

Essas ferramentas são especialmente úteis quando há suspeita de que o workflow não esteja sendo iniciado corretamente.

6. SWPR: é utilizada para reiniciar uma tarefa de Workflow após a correção do erro

 

 

Resolução dos erros

Após a identificação da causa, é possível direcionar as ações corretivas de forma mais objetiva. A seguir, são apresentados alguns dos cenários mais recorrentes na operação.

 

1. Falha na determinação do agente aprovador

Quando estamos lidando com erros relacionados à determinação do agente aprovador, existem algumas possibilidades a serem consideradas. Esse tipo de falha normalmente indica um problema na leitura da estrutura de aprovação pelo sistema.

As principais causas são:

  • O usuário que iniciou o Workflow não possui business user (verificar na transação SU01D) ou não está corretamente atribuído na estrutura do HCM ou em Times e Responsabilidades. Com isso, o sistema não consegue identificar quem está hierarquicamente acima na estrutura de aprovação;
  • O usuário aprovador não possui business user ou não está atribuído corretamente na estrutura do HCM ou em Times e Responsabilidades;
  • O aprovador possui um substituto ativo atribuído que não possui business user ou não está corretamente vinculado na estrutura do HCM ou em Times e Responsabilidades. Nesse caso, recomenda-se verificar a existência de substitutos na transação RMPS_SET_SUBSTITUTE.

 

2. Workflow não aparece na SWIA

Quando o Workflow não aparece nas atividades da SWIA, isso pode indicar alguma falha de configuração no sistema.

Nesses casos, recomenda-se verificar:

  • No customizing do documento que está sendo criado, se a ativação do Workflow Flexível foi realizada corretamente para o tipo de documento em questão;
  • Se existe alguma pendência na transação SWU3, garantindo que todos os itens estejam com o indicador em verde;
  • No aplicativo Administrar Workflows, se há pelo menos um Workflow ativo e se o documento em análise atende aos requisitos necessários para a inicialização.

 

3. Workflow não inicia

Verificar:

  • Se as condições de início do Workflow estão sendo atendidas (valor, tipo de documento, empresa, centro, etc.);
  • Se o documento está em status liberado para Workflow;
  • Se houve erro na criação dos eventos (via SWEL/SWELS);
  • Se o Workflow Flexível está ativo para o objeto específico.

 

Conclusão

Em resumo, a correta utilização das ferramentas e a análise estruturada dos erros são fundamentais para garantir o bom funcionamento do Workflow Flexível. Ao adotar uma abordagem organizada na investigação, é possível identificar as causas com mais agilidade e aplicar as correções de forma mais eficiente, contribuindo para a estabilidade dos processos no SAP.

entry.title: Re: inter company transfer of CWIP line items
entry.content: The recommended approach would be to use asset transfer postings for CWIP to maintain compliance. For POs, adopt the hybrid approach: close open POs in the transferor company and re‑issue them in the transferee company. This ensures all future deliveries and invoices flow into the surviving entity, keeping procurement and accounting clean.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – App Variants are the EASY way to control App Features
entry.content:
Hi @Leslie_Castle / @Jocelyn_Dart ,

First of all great blog, but I still have some questions or maybe more confusion about the part:

"Your technical team need to decide up-front whether Adapt UI transport requests will be client-independent (workbench request) or client-specific (customizing request). Once they have decided, all Adapt UI requests must use the same approach – otherwise you will receive errors on transport (e.g. “Sorry a technical error occurred”)"

We are currently on a S/4Hana On Premise 2023 SP 01.

We have created some app variants and transported them through our landscape, while these variants were tested on our Acceptance system the testers noticed that the active version of the app variant on Acceptance wasn't the same as the one on Development. After we took a look at what happened we saw that version 1 and 2 were saved in Customizing, and the last version 3 was saved in Workbench. On transporting, the Workbench request was transported first, and after that the Customizing making version 2 the last and active version on Acceptance.

So my confusion/questions/remarks are:

  1. On creating and publishing an app variant, the user can't tell the difference between Customizing/Workbench requests. Which means the choice may be made without full awareness. 
  2. When your Development system has 2 clients (100/200) and you create an app variant in client 100 and publish it in a Workbench or Customizing. What will the difference be? Beside the different Object Types ADVD and ADVT on transport.
  3. With an app variant you create a tile in the Technical Catalog, isn't the Technical Catalog always client independent? And so how does this relate to an app variant published in a Customizing request?

Warm regards,

Pieter Breunesse

entry.title: Re: Kyma High availability

For example, you have a own supplementary clause for SAP Hana Cloud ( https://assets.cdn.sap.com/agreements/product-use-and-support-terms/cls/en/sap-hana-cloud-supplement-english-v1-2026.pdf ).

entry.title: To Be More Effective Cybersecurity Must Learn to Think in Matrices
entry.content:
By Jay Thoden van Velzen, Technical Advisor, Office of the CSO

Cybersecurity leaders have been recommending for years to learn to speak to the business. However, I hear from many that they are still struggling to get heard in their organizations. This is puzzling, since media and industry reports on cyber incidents with significant business and operational impact and increase in regulations have put security and compliance firmly on the agenda of business leaders.

There is still a communications gap. To get to the source of this communication challenge, it is helpful to explore how different groups in organizations look at applications.

Since the word “application” is used loosely in a variety of contexts, it is important to define what we mean by it. Following Kelly Shortridge, I define an application as a combination of technical and human resources, that is, a socio-technical system that delivers a necessary (business) function.

A human resource (HR) management application, for instance, that supports employee recruitment, compensation, learning, payroll, and so on, is composed of the technical stack of infrastructure, operating systems, code and surrounding tooling, as well as the developers, operators and administrators managing its configuration, uptime, and resilience. That holds whether the application is run on-premise, is in-house developed or comes from a third party, is operated in the cloud by yourself, by a managed service provider, or through a Software-as-a-Service provider.

 

Different Perspectives

However, different groups of stakeholders look at applications very differently.

Business Users and Owners

Business owners support business functions and processes. Whether intermediated through a central IT organization or functional business owners directly, they seek a solution on the market or developed in-house, and authorize purchases, fund development and operational teams to manage it in production. They are responsible for Procurement, or Finance, or HR business processes. Or an online catalog and store.

For the application users, they need to get their jobs done. They buy and sell products and services, they hire and fire employees and contractors, they manage warehouses and production lines.

That is, this group has a business to run. They don’t care how the application achieves that, or what the technical stack is, as long as it supports them in their functional role. Their domain knowledge is functional within the vertical.

Application Developers, Operators and Administrators

Applications have operators and administrators who run the application for functional business users. They manage access and authorizations, provide user support, and manage the underlying technical stack the application relies on, such as servers and operating systems, databases, backups and relevant networking. The technical stack could be on-premise or in the cloud, whether on Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) or as full Software-as-a-Service.

The application can be in-house developed or come from a 3rd-party provider. Either way, the operators and administrators need to work with the application developers or service provider on feature requests, bug fixes, or implementing patches and upgrades to the application.

This group is still closely tied to the business function. The technical teams supporting each application will know the technical stack and functionality of their application, but not that of others. Their domain knowledge is technical within the vertical space of a specific application supporting a particular business function.

Cybersecurity Professionals

Cybersecurity is cross-functional, and in case of the application technology stack, looks at it in horizontal layers. Frameworks such as the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) describe functions horizontally, such as Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond and Recover. Cybersecurity thinks in terms of network security, cloud security, operating system hardening, identity and access management, configure-, posture- and vulnerability management, application (or product) security, and so on, and then applies that across business functions across the application space. The domain expertise accordingly is horizontal, along layers of the technology stack.

Where cybersecurity connects with business users and executives, such as security and compliance awareness programs or mandatory training, they are often not engaged specifically targeted to their business function. Even privileged access users are seen as a singular class, rather than segmented by business function criticality. Cybersecurity sees them as undifferentiated between business functions, as if they all have the same requirements.

This horizontal, layered perspective also drives the market segmentation of cybersecurity solutions, targeting different layers of the technology stack:

  • network security (firewalls, web application firewalls (WAF), intrusion detection and protection systems (IDS/IPS) or anti-Denial of Service (DoS))
  • End point detection and protection agents
  • cloud posture management
  • vulnerability scanners
  • E-mail security
  • Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) systems
  • Identity and Access Management (IAM), or zero trust access

It even has Application Security as a market category, despite lacking application-specific domain expertise.

In summary, each group has a fundamentally different view of applications:

  • Business users, owners: care about what the application does – functional focus on business benefit
  • Application developers, DevOps, operators, administrators: care about what to do to make the application work – a practical focus in service of functional requirements (including security and compliance requirements)
  • Cybersecurity professionals: care about what the application is composed of – a cross-functional technical stack focus on security and compliance

Diagram illustrating the different worldviews of application owners, users, and operators along business functions, and Cybersecurity teams who look at the world horizontally in layers of technical stacks.

The diagram above illustrates these different ways that our groups perceive their function.

 

 Misaligned Communication

There is no wrong or right. These differences in perspective are the natural outcome of performing a vertical business function or a horizontal, cross-organizational one. But it has clear implications for misaligned communication and the potential pushback against central security programs from business units. A couple of examples can illustrate this:

  • Cybersecurity sets a policy for Single Sign On (SSO) and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for application access, a solution has been acquired, and teams are expected to onboard. Several business units raised objections that they need an exception because
    • their application vendor doesn’t support the solution
    • is too old to adopt it
    • the developer has left but the application still provides a business function, or
    • they’re a new acquisition and have a completely different technical stack and a competitive solution is in place
  • A business unit always misses their vulnerability management target on their application and escalations from cybersecurity are frequently denied, because patching requires a reboot that would disrupt critical business operations
  • To be more agile and resilient and save cost, a business unit decides to adopt a cloud solution. Since they want to move fast, they didn’t engage the cybersecurity team in advance, because “they just slow things down”. During implementation, the security team comes with a long checklist of requirement, delaying adoption of the solution.
  • Application teams taking it upon themselves to secure their applications while the cybersecurity team is mostly concerned with their OS and network logs and configuration
  • Cybersecurity never managing to get the budget their need from the business

This miscommunication persists even now that business executives through their professional networks and media are increasingly aware of the potential business impact of security and compliance incidents, if not direct experience.

 

Cybersecurity Must Learn To Think in Matrices

Cybersecurity must learn to think in matrices. That is, cybersecurity must learn to apply their horizontal domain expertise to the different business functional verticals to properly understand the context of what they are trying to protect. Teams must be sensitive to both the variety of technology stacks in their organizations and the value to the business functions these applications and their operating teams provide. “Enabling the business” means to enable the business functions to operate securely, not only to secure their technology stacks.

Cybersecurity mustn’t lose its horizontal perspective. Central governance, policies and services are more effective and efficient than fragmented and different in each business unit. But without the additional understanding of the vertical perspective cybersecurity can’t be optimally effective either.

At SAP, this matrixed cybersecurity function is achieved through the central-and federated Business Information Security Officer (BISO) model. This consists of a central Security and Compliance organization that sets policies and standards, mandates security solutions, runs central security programs and services, security and exception management, and provides central governance. This is paired with BISO teams within the different business units responsible for ensuring security and compliance requirements are implemented and followed in their unit. Together it creates a continuous dialogue between the central organization and the business units, where the BISO teams help provide the context and constraints of their specific vertical and the cyber teams provide the horizontal domain expertise.

 

Recommendations

Learning To Listen

Setting up a matrixed operating model doesn’t work if we don’t learn to listen, or it just becomes another forum to talk past each other. Central cybersecurity teams can often dismiss any objection by business units as an excuse not to do something, and inevitably that is sometimes the case. But in many cases, there are real constraints that should be acknowledged. It’s hard to convince a business unit that a potential but not materialized risk must be mitigated with urgency when doing so causes business disruptions with a guaranteed price tag.

Quantitative Security Risk Management as an Aid

Quantitative security risk management can be an effective communication aid that works both ways. It is easier to articulate the probability of real business impact if the security risk is not remediated, and it justifies the cost of remediation in relation to the risk. But it also educates and disciplines the cybersecurity team on what is critical and important to the business, and what is therefore more important to protect. This can be enlightening, but also sobering, if the plan to address the latest hyper cybersecurity trend proves to cost too much relative to the risk it is meant to address.

More on how SAP uses quantitative security risk management can be found in this article.

Cloud Transformation and Platformization as Opportunities

Cloud transformation and the trend of platformization in software and services provides further opportunities. Technology stacks can be simplified both horizontally from the bottom up - through use of IaaS - and vertically - through moving from spot solutions to integrated platforms and application suites. By doing so, we make the matrix simpler, reducing complexity. Cloud transformation reduces the depth of the technology stack by outsourcing more responsibilities to cloud providers. Platformization reduces the width and variety of the technology stack by reducing the number of different solutions to support, operate and integrate.

Look Beyond the Tech Stack

Applications have a business function. They are not only a collection of code, infrastructure, network configurations and open-source libraries that may be vulnerable to SQL injections, cross-site scripting, or directory traversal. To secure applications, and not just the components in the technology stack, requires domain knowledge of the application. Otherwise, we miss what the application is for, how it and its business logic can be abused, and what impact such abuse would have on the business function it supports.

SAP and the partner ecosystem provide security solutions with application domain expertise, that is, they are aware of what the application is for, and how it can be abused, within the context of the application and business function it provides. Explore if similar solutions exist for your other applications.

Even further, cybersecurity must understand the social aspects of the application as well – the constraints that developer and operations teams are under – for instance in terms of budget and headcount. Security teams also must have a sense of how application users use it and how it helps them perform their business function. When users bypass your security controls, sit with those users to understand what they need to do to do their job.

Finally, understanding the challenges and motivations of those in the business unit makes the communication with the business owners much easier.

This is how we get better.

 

More Information

For more information, see:

  • More Effective Security Programs Through Security Risk Quantification
  • How SAP Security Teams Can Help Cyber Teams Towards Cyber Resilience
  • Cyber Resilience and Cloud Transformation: Resolving the ERP Security Hamster Wheel

entry.title: 专业服务-外部员工服务采购
entry.content:
业务需求

外部员工在以服务为中心的行业中发挥着重要作用，SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云为专业服务场景中的客户项目提供了两种采购这些外部临时员工的方式，基于项目或者基于成本中心。本博文介绍案例基于项目的采购外部员工服务。

参考博文

英文博文：External Contingent Workforce for Customer Project... - SAP Community

前提配置

1.APP:管理劳动力，创建外部员工，员工类型选择：BBP005，字段临时工填写对应采购订单的供应商比如10300001。

创建工作协议

进入详细页面，设置服务级别

2.物料主数据

由于DEMO DATA只安装与初始系统中，所以请参考初始系统来创建对应的主数据，以物料T001为例，注意：物料组请默认设定P001，不能选择自定义物料组。

3.配置点：Define expense (101306)，完成需要用到的科目分配

4.APP:管理成本要素组

5.配置点：maintain billing profile(103637)

系统实现

1.创建客户项目

详细请参考博文：专业服务端到端最佳业务实践介绍 - SAP Community

注意：团队角色T001对应采购订单的物料

案例选择固定价格开票类型

转到APP：检查客户项目

2.创建采购订单

在采购订单行项目里，注意以下：

  • 物料选择T001对应客户项目里的角色
  • 科目分配类型选择项目(P)
  • 服务执行者，这里对应管理劳动力外部员工Z00002的前置配置点
  • 总账科目自动带出
  • 增加字段WBS元素，并且维护WBS

收货选项默认勾上，不能去掉。调整执行日期，这个时间段决定了你做报工的时间段。然后保存采购订单3.APP: 发送缺少时间通知（Notify Missing Time），对外部员工做人天报工审批报工

当报工审批后，系统会自动对采购订单进行收货，并且生成Service Entry Sheet。

4.回到APP: 检查客户项目，查看实际成本5.后续对采购订单做发票和销售开票，这里不进行演示。

多谢观看

entry.title: Onward Replication of Business Partner master data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP landscapes, Business Partner data consistency across systems is critical for smooth business operations. With growing hybrid system landscapes – especially involving SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public and Private Editions – data replication has become more complex.

This blog explains the current challenges with Business Partner SOAP/Web Services, the workarounds used today, and how Onward Replication in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a future-ready solution for seamless data distribution. This blog is co-written with @Karunaharan.

 

Current Situation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

In the current setup, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition typically participates in point-to-point data replication. In this setup, the system is capable of only point-to-point replication, meaning it acts as a sender or receiver of master data with one connected system at a time. 

For example, consider three systems: 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Currently: 

  • When master data is created or changed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it is replicated to both C4C and  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. 
  • But when master data is created or changed in C4C, it is replicated only to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
  • Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is treated only as a receiver in this setup, it does not send the C4C changes further to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

 

Common Issues

  • No automatic onward replication.
  • Additional integration logic required.
  • Manual handling increases error risk.
  • Data consistency is harder to maintain across systems.                                                                 

Workarounds Used Today

To overcome these limitations, customers often implement custom workarounds.

Typical Workaround:

Business data from C4C is sent to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via the SAP Integration Suite middleware. Once C4C receives confirmation, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system triggers an OData call to enable a custom flag, which triggers replication to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

Drawbacks of Workarounds

  • Increased development and maintenance effort
  • Custom logic is hard to scale
  • Higher chances of data inconsistencies
  • Not aligned with SAP’s clean core strategy

 

The Solution: Onward Replication in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP addresses these challenges with Onward Replication, allowing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to act as a data forwarding system.

What Is Onward Replication?

Onward Replication enables forwarding of Business Partner data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to multiple target systems as soon as the data is received or created. This feature is available starting from the 2602 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Activating Onward Replication for Business Partner

Onward Replication is disabled by default. It must be enabled via a dedicated configuration activity.

Typical Workflow

  1. Define target systems for replication
  2. Access the configuration activity in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  3. Enable Onward Replication for Business Partner
  4. Monitor replication status via standard tools

Once activated, SAP handles the forwarding - no custom flags or middleware logic required.

Key Features and Improvements

Onward replication brings several benefits to SAP landscapes:

  • Data Forwarding
    Ensures business partner data reaches all required systems
  • Real-Time Consistency
    Keeps master data aligned across systems
  • Optimal Performance
    Reduces duplicate processing and custom logic
  • Reduced Manual Effort
    Eliminates workaround-based development
  • Support for Multiple Targets
    One source, many consumers

 

Practical Example :

  • Business Partner is created in C4C
  • Data reaches SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition replicates the same data to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

With Onward Replication enabled:

  • The business partner object is created once.
  • The system forwards it to all configured target systems.
  • No additional integration steps or custom triggers are needed.

 

From Point-to-Point Integration to Hub-Like Model

With the 2602 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system evolves from a point-to-point replication framework into a central hub:

  • Acts as a master data distribution centre
  • Seamlessly integrates with multiple SAP and non-SAP systems
  • Supports SAP’s long-term cloud and clean core strategy

This shift significantly simplifies enterprise-wide master data governance.

 

Conclusion

Onward Replication is a game-changing capability for Business Partner data management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It eliminates complex workarounds, reduces integration effort, and ensures real-time data consistency across systems.

For SAP consultants, developers, and business users, enabling Onward Replication is a crucial step toward a simpler, scalable, and future-ready SAP integration architecture.

entry.title: SAP PO - BPM error
entry.content:
Price Tolerance is completed but has not reflected in Finance, even though it was already approved. When we checked in the Fiori system, we observed that the Price Tolerance approval for PO is still pending. It was queried multiple times and all previous queries were approved, but the last approval was not completed successfully.

Upon investigation, we found that the BPM task was suspended and in error. When we checked the reason for the error, we found the following comments:

------------------------
"Process 'ApprovalRefProcess' suspended Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' set to In Error Mapping failed Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' suspended Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' set to In Error Mapping failed Process 'ApprovalCO' suspended Mapping failed java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Value 'KZN Durban Storage' 9 boxes Xtraspace, 99 Chris Hani Road, Briadene, Durban, 4051 Lionel Thangamuthu 060 588 5815 (CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Sep 23 2025 15:12:35)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Oct 28 2025 06:59:55)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Wed Oct 29 2025 11:18:07)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Oct 31 2025 07:49:25)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Nov 04 2025 08:03:29)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Thu Nov 06 2025 13:11:28)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 07 2025 11:53:11)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 14 2025 11:23:08)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Nov 17 2025 08:39:31)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Nov 18 2025 08:46:46)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 21 2025 10:12:19)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Nov 24 2025 10:16:41)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Thu Nov 27 2025 08:43:33)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 28 2025 11:50:02)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Dec 01 2025 07:26:45) of type http://www.sap.com/caf/oup.com/fpa.bl.serreq.modeled#LongText fails validation check: MaxLength=1100 com.sap.mapping.lib.execution.api.exception.TExecutionException: Cannot process assignment RelayTransformer, VoidExpander( TerminalPart.assignment ) at ReqInternalText( TerminalPart.assignment ) at Header( BroadPart.child ) at updateRequest( NarrowPart.child ) at com.sap.caf.oup.com/fpa.bl.serreq.oup/com/caf/app/serr/serreqservice:updateHeaderItemDataV1( BroadPart.child ) at ( NarrowMapping ) For more details, decrease severity of log controller as described in SAP Note 1755360. ---------------------------------------------------------------------

We identified that the failure occurred due to the maximum length validation error. To resolve this, we edited the PO 'header note' in ME22N in SAP and tried to resume the BPM task, but it failed again for the same reason. We then asked the user to resubmit the Price Tolerance for approval, but in the Fiori page, the option to submit for approval was disabled. To address this, we canceled the BPM task for the PO.

Requirement - User need to get this Price Tolerance approved and it should be reflected in Finance.

entry.title: Re: 无法找到采购订单中物料数据页签中组件增强
entry.content:
Good day @jayden_glamour 

Thank you for your questions.

Based on your screenshot, the Components button is for a subcontracting purchase order item, which belong to the Category L. My understanding is that you want to update the subcontracting component information, but you cannot find an enhancement spot for the Material Data Components area.

Please note that in Public Cloud, this Components area is not available/exposed. That explains why you can find enhancements for tabs like Scheduling or Account Assignment but not for Components.

You can also not open it via Expert Configuration in Public Cloud because it does not allow you to enable new enhancement spots for that UI area through configuration.

See https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3558790

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3aa57e5b9a9f46218ef8cc38d703c8f4.html  

I suggest you consider supported alternatives the public cloud provides. Such as:

Option 1: Maintaining components via standard UI.  See KBA 2909027 for more details.

If your business only needs to adjust components manually, then see the following quick guide:

Open the purchase order in Manage Purchase Orders, Select the subcontracting item, go to Material Data, click components, maintain the component list that belongs to the visible standard fields and save it.

go to Material Data, click components, maintain the component list that belongs to the visible standard fields and save it.


Option 2: Alternatively, you can maintain components as the source. In most contracting processes, PO components are derived from BOM/subcontracting master data. I suggest:

  • Maintain the correct BOM or relevant master data
  • Recreate the PO item or triggers redetermination if available in your tenant.
  • Then the PO components are proposed correctly from the start.

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to create custom field with URL?
entry.content:
Hey guys, 

create a QCF with type Freeform and set it to non-editable. Via script  you can insert the URL, e.g. use event on quote created

urltag = "<a href=\"https://google.de\">Hier steht anstatt Text eine URL</a>"

context.Quote["QuoteCustomFieldName"] = urltag

BR,

Tobias

ℹ️ What this session is about

Learn how SAP’s Commodity Management solution, tightly integrated with our ERP, helps customers grow earnings and manage risk by fully leveraging assets across integrated value chains. The session will illustrate how traders and supply-chain teams can capture value across physical and financial flows, and show where integrated processes deliver the fastest business impact.

entry.title: Hierarchical presentations of dimension XY are not supported in a blended chart
entry.content:
I am developing a Budget Profit & Loss (P&L) Report in SAC that is required to be supported by an import scheduled job. The data will be coming from two data sources i.e. BW and Excel (organized as per the required layout).

I created two (2) models from the above-mentioned data sources. For the model, with data coming from the excel file, I made level 1, level 2, level 3 to be properties of Level 4 and created a level-based hierarchy. See sample excel file below:Note: The workaround of introducing a new dimension (Code) by duplicating the dimension ‘Level 4’ is an attempt to resolve the error message: "Hierarchical presentations of dimension XY are not supported in a blended chart"

I went ahead to design the report in SAC Stories, by blending the above model using dimension: Code with the BW model using the dimension: GL code. This approach allows me to schedule or automate the import job.

See below the output:

However, the desired output is this:

Previously, I had merged the models rather than blending to achieve the above desired output. This approach prevents me from scheduling or automating the import job which is desirable by the customer. See screenshot below, the import job button (highlighted in red) is greyed out.

Note: I get the same result when I upload the excel file from my local file or from Google drive.

In view of this, how do I achieve the desired output and still ensure that I can also schedule the import job especially coming from BW system?

entry.title: Delay "Create Task" in a trigger-based campaign
entry.content:
Hi, 

We have the following case in SAP Marketing Cloud.

We have a Form in which a Sample can be requested. We want to create a Task with a specific category "Sample Request" in SAP Sales Cloud for that. However, Tasks can only be created on existing business partner. And the form can be filled in by completely new contacts as well. So, first a Lead needs to be sent to SAP Sales Cloud, which in its turn will already create an Account and a Contact with status "in preparation" and send the BUPA ID's back to SAP Mkt Cloud. From that moment on, it is technically possible for SMC to send a "Task" to Sales Cloud because the BUPA ids exist. 

So, ideally, I have a trigger-based campaign that triggers on the Form Submit (on the form there are some custom fields that are sent to CPI as Predecessor Interaction information), as a first action a "Create Lead", and as a second action a couple of minutes later, a "Create Task". But I am unable to make a setup like that. It's not possible to add the "Create Task" action after the create lead, and it's also not possible to add a delay between the trigger and the "create task" action.

I already tried adding a kind of dummy email in between, with then a follow-up trigger and connect both the Yes and No - wait 1 hour" branch to the "Create Task" action. But then this "send email" becomes the Predecessor Interaction and in CPI the custom fields from the Form Submit trigger are no longer coming through... 

 

Any ideas on other ways to set this up are welcome!

entry.title: Upgrading PowerDesiger 16.7 SP06 to 16.7 SP09
entry.content:
Hello,

When upgrading PowerDesigner 16.7 SP06 to 16.7 SP09, do we also need to update the license server?
Are the floating licenses we currently have on our license server (16.7 sp06-pl02) valid, or will we need to request new licenses?
If we update to this new version, will the repository database be updated? How many changes does this version include?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Upgrading PowerDesiger 16.7 SP06 to 16.7 SP09
entry.content:
Hello,

PD 16.7 SP09 requires SySAM 2.5.0, which is also included in the SP09 archive file.
All PD 16.7 licenses are compatible with every PD 16.7 SP and PL levels, including PD 16.7 SP09. Therefore, you do not need to generate a new license (.lic) file.

When the ADMIN connects to the repository from PD 16.7 SP09 for the first time, they will be prompted to upgrade the repository definitions.
Consequently, any user running PD 16.7 SP06 will no longer be able to connect to the repository. All users must be running the same SP level.

You can find details about the new features in the What's New Summary:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_POWERDESIGNER/60c5b6387b7049c997d4e13782a34a43/b58f4166cfbf47b5a24d354d2538918a.html?locale=en-US&version=16.7.09&state=PRODUCTION

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Updating a custom field of std app with a custom app...
entry.content:
Hi @ayechan 

If I understood your question correctly, you want to update a custom field in a standard application.
Since you’re using Developer Extensibility, you can use the EML local API to update this field.

Here’s an example:

ENTITY salescontract
  UPDATE 
    FIELDS ( YY1_custom_uuid_32_sc_SDH ) 
    WITH VALUE #( ( YY1_custom_uuid_32_sc_SDH = 'NEW UUID'
                    %key-salescontract        = '0000004800' ) )
  FAILED   DATA(ls_failed)
  REPORTED DATA(ls_reported).

In this example, the custom field YY1_custom_uuid_32_sc_SDH is updated for a specific sales contract.
Did I understand your question correctly?
Did this example help you?

BR, Oleg

entry.title: Re: EWM: How is the Quantity field of an HU derived?
entry.content:
Hi,

in EWM you should use SAP-Standard function modules / classes instead of reading the Data directly from the databases itself. This ensures that the Joins / connection to relevant databases are done correctly and the SAP-Standard functions also buffer data. Generally there is a very good book which contains some technical data for EWM-Devs (EWM with SAP S/4HANA: Architecture and Programming) This could help you with processing HUs / Delivery and a lot of other EWM-Objects.

 

For reading HU-Items / Quantities you should use the Function module /SCWM/HU_READ instead of directly select your data based on the tables you have found. The quantities are stored within the exporting parameter ET_HUITM of the FM.

 

For just seeing the quantity within a transaction you can also use the /SCWM/MON. This is a very important transaction in EWM and offers you a lot functions. Within the MON a node "stock and bin" is defined which contains the sub-node "Handling Unit". When you click it 2 times a selection screen will open where you can insert your HU-Number. Afterwards you see some general information to your HU e.g. creation date / HU-Type (database behind is the /SCWM/HUHDR) and within the top bar you have the button "phys. stock" which will load the physical stock to your HU.

You might need to scroll to right to see all regions. At present, as you also pointed out the SLA is 99.95%. There are no current plans to provide 99.99%. It may change in the future. For rest, please ensure that your applications are also highly available, you can set up multiple replicas across different availability zones. https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/resilient-applications-in-kyma-environment

entry.title: Re: Freight Unit Type Determination with DG Profile via B...
entry.content:
Why the wrong FU type is picked is because

  • FU Type Determination is Header‑Driven: The FU type is determined once per FU building run, not per item. If multiple items are bundled into one FU, only one FU type is applied.
  • DG Profile at Item Level: BRF+ can evaluate item attributes (DG profile, product category, etc.), but if FU building groups items together, the FU type determination logic only sees the “first” or “dominant” item.
  • Default FU Type Fallback: If no valid FU type is found for the combination, the system falls back to the default FU type maintained in customizing.

So the recommended approach would be to configure FU building rules to separate items by DG profile. Once items are split, BRF+ FU type determination will correctly assign FU Type 1 for DG items and FU Type 2 for non‑DG items. You achieve DG vs Non‑DG separation by adding DG profile as a grouping attribute in FU building rules. This forces the system to create separate FUs, and then BRF+ FU type determination applies correctly to each FU.

entry.title: Bringing AI Context to Eclipse ADT: A Local MCP Setup Guide
entry.content:
The Context Gap

Recently, I came across an insightful article from Alice Vinogradova explaining how to use Claude Code to revolutionize our ABAP workflow. While I agree that tools like Claude Code are fantastic for coding, many of us face a common reality: corporate restrictions.

Like me, you might be working in an environment where you can't access external AI tools or install unauthorized software, and you are eagerly waiting for the SAP Toolkit to fully support VS Code.

But I do have access to Eclipse ADT and I’ve managed to get GitHub Copilot running. That got me thinking: What if we could use Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to bridge the gap between our locked-down SAP systems and our AI agents?

Boom! It turns out, we can. By deploying MCP servers locally, we can safely expose our ABAP context and documentation to Copilot without breaking security protocols.

Here is how I set up my "Old School" local build to get full AI context inside Eclipse.

Step 1: The Build (Old School Style)

I prefer building from source. It allows me to track exactly what dependencies are being installed and analyze the code—crucial for maintaining a clean and secure corporate machine.

I created a local folder called MCPs to house my helpers.

1. Vibing Steampunk (The ADT Bridge)

First, we need the bridge that connects the AI to our SAP system via the ADT interface.

Bash
 
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/oisee/vibing-steampunk.git
cd vibing-steampunk

# Build the binary
make build

2. SAP Documentation (The Knowledge Base)

Next, I added the SAP Docs MCP server (by marianfoo) to the mix. This tool enriches the AI's responses with official documentation, ensuring it doesn't hallucinate non-existent syntax.

#Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/marianfoo/mcp-sap-docs.git
cd mcp-sap-docs 
# From repo root
npm ci
./setup.sh # execute this script to clone the github documentation submodules
npm run build

Step 2: Configuring Eclipse

Once the builds are complete, we need to tell the Eclipse Copilot agent where to find these new tools.

  1. Open Eclipse.

  2. Navigate to the Model Context Protocol (MCP) configuration section in Github Copilot Chat:

  3. Add the following JSON configuration.

Note: Update the paths (/Users/xxx/...) and credentials to match your local setup.

{
	"servers": {
		"sap-docs": {
			"command": "node",
			"args": [
				"/Users/xxx/MCPs/mcp-sap-docs/dist/src/server.js"
			]
		},
		"abap-adt-command": {
			"type": "stdio",
			"command": "/Users/xxx/MCPs/vibing-steampunk/build/vsp",
			"args": [
				"--url",
				"https://vxxx.frx.hec.xxx.com:44300",
				"--user",
				"USER",
				"--password",
				"PASSWORD",
				"--client",
				"100"
			]
		}
	}
}

If everything is configured correctly, checking the Tools section in the Agent view will list all available capabilities (like search_sap_docs, get_transport, etc.).

Pro Tip: For performance reasons, deactivate any tools you don't use often to keep the context window clean.

Step 3: The Results

With the setup ready, I put the agent to the test with some real-world ABAP scenarios.

Scenario A: The On-Call SRE

I asked the agent to act as a Site Reliability Engineer and analyze the system state.

Prompt: "You are an on-call ABAP SRE. Investigate recent dumps, trace the execution path, and identify the most likely root cause. Check if there are relevant SAP notes or community blogs about these problems"

The Result: The agent was able to pull actual dumps from the system and summarize them into a professional report:

I'll shorten the answer for narrative proposes:

I'll analyze the three recent ST22 dumps you provided, trace the call stacks, give the most likely root cause for each, point to relevant docs/community leads, and provide immediate and next-step remediation actions.

Checklist (what I did)
- [x] Fetched recent dumps (ST22 HTML pages you provided)
- [x] Extracted key header + call-stack + source-lines for each dump
- [x] Reasoned the most likely root cause for each dump (code bug vs infra)
- [x] Looked up SAP Help and SAP Community references that are relevant / actionable
- [x] Produced immediate mitigations, repro/diagnosis steps, and recommended long-term fixes

Summary of dumps inspected
- ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY — /XXX/CL_TCORE_DATA_BUFFER=====CP READ_ORDERS — 2026-01-02 15:33:17 — Background job on host vxxx_xxx_00 (client xxx). Terminating statement: INSERT ls_order_info INTO TABLE gt_order_info (include CM001 line ~115).
- CALL_FUNCTION_ACCEPT_FAILED — SAPMSSY1 REMOTE_FUNCTION_CALL — 2026-01-02 09:07:12 — CPIC/RFC error: CM_SECURITY_NOT_VALID / RFC_IO5 (SNC-related). Termination in CALL 'RfcImport' ID 'Type' (include SAPMSSY1 line ~185).
- TIME_OUT — CL_WDR_CHGTOOL_MD_READER======CP REC_GET_CONTEXT_INFO — 2026-01-02 10:46:29 — Program exceeded uninterrupted runtime (profile parameter rdisp/scheduler/prio_high/max_runtime = 660s). Termination during looping over metadata attributes (include CM003 around line ~62).

Detailed analysis and next steps

1) ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY — /PPA/CL_TCORE_DATA_BUFFER=====CP :: READ_ORDERS
- Evidence (from dump)
- Runtime error: ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY (CX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY)
- Termination at: INSERT ls_order_info INTO TABLE gt_order_info (highlighted line)
- Key component values shown: {004030000389;0020;} (these are the key values that caused the duplicate)
- Call stack: READ_ORDERS -> CHECK_ORDERS -> GET_ACCNT_SERVICE_ORDER -> WRITE_ACCNT_SERVICE_ORDER -> WRITE_ACCNT_OBJECTS -> ... (main SAPMSSYC)
- Program started as a background job.
- Likely root cause
- The code attempts to insert a row into an internal table that has a unique (primary or unique secondary) key that already exists. The INSERT statement here is not handling duplicates and the table type / key composition causes ABAP to raise CX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY and abort.
- This is most often a logic bug: either the incoming data contains duplicates, or prior logic already inserted the same key into gt_order_info earlier in the loop, and the code does not test for existence before INSERT.
- Less likely: a race between parallel processes — internal tables are process-local, so race conditions across WPs are not applicable unless gt_order_info is a static/shared object (e.g., attribute of a shared object or using shared memory). From the stack it looks like a normal local table in a background job.
- Recommended immediate mitigations
- Change the INSERT to either:
- Check existence before insert:
- READ TABLE gt_order_info WITH TABLE KEY <key fields> INTO <some> and only INSERT if not present; or
- Use INSERT ... INTO TABLE ... ACCEPTING DUPLICATE KEYS (if duplicates are benign and you can ignore them), or
- Use INSERT ... INTO TABLE ... TRANSPORTING to avoid delayed unique key update issues as appropriate.
- Add logging around the insertion (user, key values, call context) to capture which input produced duplicate keys.
- Reproduction / diagnosis steps
- Inspect declaration of `gt_order_info` to determine key fields and whether it is SORTED / HASHED / STANDARD with UNIQUE secondary keys.
- Run the code in the debugger on a copy of the same input or add an explicit READ before INSERT to capture the duplicate values and reproduce.
- If background job: re-run job in test system or with a small controlled dataset to confirm fix.
- Long-term fix
- Make the code robust to duplicate input (explicit checks, deduplication before loop, or use internal-table operations intended for possible duplicates).
- Add a unit test that simulates duplicate input.
- Useful references
- ABAP docs on INSERT/ITAB and duplicate keys (explanations and examples of when exception is thrown):
- INSERT into internal table / duplicates: ABAP docs (INSERT itab / duplicate behaviour) — see ABAP docs pages on INSERT and itab unique/secondary keys.
- SAP Community threads with examples/diagnostics of ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY (search term: "CX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY INSERT INTO TABLE") — common examples and fixes.

 

...

...

Relevant SAP docs / community resources I found (quick list)
- ABAP docs on runtime errors and exceptions (general guide): ABAP runtime errors / exception classes — useful to understand DX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY semantics.
- ABAP docs - INSERT / internal table duplicate behavior: explanation of when ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY is raised and examples of safe patterns (READ before INSERT, ACCEPTING DUPLICATE KEYS).
- SAP Community threads (examples and discussions):
- Examples where INSERT into internal tables caused CX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY and how developers wrapped INSERT in try/catch or used READ TABLE to avoid crash.
- General posts on TIME_OUT and rdisp scheduler parameters (how max_runtime is used and default values).
- For RFC/SNC issues:
- SAP Help pages and configuration guides on SNC, SM59, and RFC troubleshooting (look for "Secure Network Communication", "SM59 test connection", "RFC_IO5", "CM_SECURITY_NOT_VALID").
- If you need SAP OSS notes for a specific kernel/patch-level interaction or a specific RFC error, collect the trace files and open an OSS incident — the low-level RFC I/O errors often need kernel-level investigation.

(Notes about search results)
- I searched SAP docs and community for specific error combinations; the ABAP docs (INSERT / internal table documentation) and community posts about ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY are directly relevant and available in SAP Help and community - those explain behavior and coding patterns to avoid the error.
- For the SNC/RFC error I did not find a single canonical OSS note in the returned results that exactly matches CM_SECURITY_NOT_VALID + RFC_IO5; SNC failures are typically environment-specific (certs, SNC library, partner names), so targeted SM59 and gateway traces are normally required before finding an OSS note. If you want I can run a focused query on SAP OSS once you provide any kernel level logs or RFC destination names.

Immediate action plan you can run now (ordered)
1. ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY
- Open ST22 for that dump and capture the full dump ID, active variables. (You already have HTML; include dump ID in any ticket.)
- Inspect the code: open class /PPA/CL_TCORE_DATA_BUFFER and check the declaration of `gt_order_info` and the preceding loop that fills ls_order_info. Add a READ TABLE check or use "ACCEPTING DUPLICATE KEYS" where appropriate.
- Add defensive logging right before the INSERT to capture duplicates (key values and call context).
- If feasible, reproduce in a test client with the same input set.

...

...

What to collect and provide to SAP OSS (if you open a ticket)
- For ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY: ST22 short dump text (full HTML), code snippet of the affected include/class, description of input dataset and job configuration.
- For RFC/SNC (CALL_FUNCTION_ACCEPT_FAILED): SM59 test results, SM21 logs, gateway trace (gwrdump/gwserv trace), RFC trace, dump HTML, and RFC destination name.
- For TIME_OUT: ST22 dump, SAT/ST12 profiling trace, number of attributes processed, input dataset, and job schedule.

 

Scenario B: Code Refactoring

I then asked it to modernize a legacy report (z_lsn_test_2).

Prompt: "You are a senior ABAP refactoring assistant. Review report zlsn_test_2. Find DSAG ABAP guidelines for object-oriented programming that could be used here and also add syntax for inline declarations in ABAP 7.58. And then apply the changes with minimal, well-documented edits. "

After several tool usage:

Perfect! The refactoring is complete and activated. Let me provide you with a final summary:
✅ZLSN_TEST_2 Refactoring Complete
I have successfully refactored ZLSN_TEST_2 with modern ABAP 7.58 syntax and DSAG OOP guidelines. The refactored program is now active in your system.

Key Refactoring Changes:

1. ABAP 7.58 Modern Syntax Applied
•  ✅ Inline Declarations (DATA) - Variables declared at point of use within loops and SPLIT operations
•  ✅ Functional VALUE Constructor - Single consolidated statements replacing field-by-field assignments
•  ✅ Modern SELECT Syntax - ABAP SQL with inline declarations for database reads
•  ✅ Safe Table Lookups - VALUE #(...) with OPTIONAL clause to prevent exceptions


2. DSAG OOP Guidelines Applied

Single Responsibility Principle:
| Procedure | Purpose | Lines | |-----------|---------|-------|
| read_file_records() | File I/O via GUI_UPLOAD | ~35 |
| parse_and_validate_records() | Parse CSV and validate data | ~25 |
| process_updates() | Coordinate database update | ~30 |
| handle_test_mode() | Display test output | ~5 |
| handle_live_mode() | Execute update & confirm | ~8 |

Composition Pattern:

•  Delegated responsibilities to focused procedures
•  Clear separation: file I/O → parsing → validation → database update → output

Fail-Fast Principle:

•  Input validation happens immediately after file read
•  Early exit prevents wasting resources on invalid data

3. Code Structure Improvements

Before:
•  1 monolithic START-OF-SELECTION block (100+ lines)
•  Mixed concerns: file I/O, parsing, validation, database update, output
•  Difficult to test, maintain, understand
•  High cyclomatic complexity
After:
•  5 focused procedures (avg. 20 lines each)
•  Clear separation of concerns
•  Each procedure independently testable
•  75% reduction in nesting depth

4. Status
✅Program activated successfully
✅Syntax check passed (1 warning - not critical)
✅Functionality preserved - All original features intact
✅100% DSAG compliant - Following proven SAP community guidelines
✅Ready for production - Can be deployed immediately


Summary of Improvements
| Aspect | Impact | |--------|--------|
| Code Clarity | 90% improved - Clear procedure names and responsibilities |
| Maintainability | 85% improved - Easy to find and modify specific functionality |
| Testability | From 0% to 100% - Each procedure can be tested independently |
| Performance | No degradation - Inline declarations compile to identical code |
| Compliance | 100% - Full DSAG + ABAP 7.58 best practices |

ZLSN_TEST_2 has now been refactored and documented with comprehensive technical details following DSAG guidelines and ABAP 7.58 modern syntax! 🎉

Conclusion

By chaining these two MCP servers together, we turn Eclipse into a powerhouse. vibing-steampunk gives the AI "eyes" to see our specific system state, while mcp-sap-docs gives it the "brain" of the official documentation.

It’s a secure, local, and highly effective way to level up your ABAP development—even behind the corporate firewall.

What's next?

So far I think it's a big improvements with the reduced scope of these two tools. But at the same time, there are still things that can be tweaked:

  • Agent customizing: I'm using here out-of-the-box components. With more fine tuning the responses would be much better.
  • Tool improvements: as you know, there are some restrictions when using Eclipse with ABAP. And this is also dependent on the Eclipse, ADT or Copilot version. With a specific version combination of them, the behavior would be much better and less erratic.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Hamburg
entry.content:
Moin liebe SAP Community Hamburg!

die DSAG‑Technologietage (17.–18.03.)  stehen vor der Tür und finden diesmal sogar in Hamburg statt! Dafür laden wir euch am 16.03.2026 herzlich zu einem besonderen SAP Stammtisch Hamburg ein zur gemeinsamen Einstimmung auf zwei spannende Tage voller SAP-Technologie.

Vor Ort ist unser Kollege Timo John, der sich schon sehr auf einen lebendigen Austausch, spannende Gespräche und einen gemütlichen Abend mit euch freut.

Wichtig: Bitte meldet euch vorab kurz an, für die Planung.

Wir freuen uns auf euch – und auf einen inspirierenden Start in die DSAG‑Technologietage!

entry.title: Re: Method 'TIMESHEETENTRYSE_UPDATE_ENTITY' not implement...
entry.content:
Hello @renaye ,

I tried to call an external API https://api.sap.com/api/API_MANAGE_WORKFORCE_TIMESHEET in ABAP Class to update Timesheet Record and it's working as expected, there is my code example:

TRY.
    DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_comm_arrangement(
      comm_scenario  = 'YY1_TIMESHEEET'
      service_id     = 'YY1_TIMESHEET_REST'
      comm_system_id = 'XXX'
    ).
    DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( lo_destination ).
    DATA(lo_request) = lo_http_client->get_http_request( ).

    lo_request->set_uri_path( '/TimeSheetEntryCollection' ).
    lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'X-CSRF-Token' i_value = 'fetch' ).

    DATA(lo_res_get) = lo_http_client->execute( if_web_http_client=>get ).
    DATA(lv_token)   = lo_http_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>get )->get_header_field( 'X-CSRF-Token' ).

    lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'Content-Type' i_value = 'application/json' ).
    lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'DataServiceVersion' i_value = '2.0' ).
    lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'Accept' i_value = 'application/json' ).
    lo_request->set_uri_path( '/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MANAGE_WORKFORCE_TIMESHEET/TimeSheetEntryCollection' ).
    lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'X-CSRF-Token' i_value = lv_token ).
    lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'x-api-key' i_value = 'q3NlqpAhNGiT9ubG1atool5AfPtEIKYA' ).

    lo_request->set_text(
      i_text =
        '{"TimeSheetDataFields": {' &&
        '"ControllingArea": "A000",' &&
        '"SenderCostCenter": "17101902",' &&
        '"ReceiverCostCenter": "",' &&
        '"InternalOrder": "",' &&
        '"ActivityType": "T002",' &&
        '"WBSElement": "DENS1.1.1",' &&
        '"RecordedHours": "1",' &&
        '"RecordedQuantity": "1",' &&
        '"HoursUnitOfMeasure": "H",' &&
        '"RejectionReason": ""' &&
        '},' &&
        '"PersonWorkAgreementExternalID": "DENNISL",' &&
        '"CompanyCode": "1710",' &&
        '"TimeSheetRecord": "000000000211",' &&
        '"PersonWorkAgreement": "50000000",' &&
        '"TimeSheetDate": "/Date(1687132800000)/",' &&
        '"TimeSheetOperation": "U"' &&
        '}'
    ).

    DATA(lo_res_post) = lo_http_client->execute( if_web_http_client=>post ).
    DATA(lv_text) = lo_res_post->get_text( ).

  CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_error).
ENDTRY.

 I've created Custom Communication Scenario YY1_TIMESHEEET via Fiori app 'Custom Communication Scenarios', it's needed to add Outbound Service with next URL path: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MANAGE_WORKFORCE_TIMESHEET. Publish Communication Scenario and based on it create a Communication Arrangement with Communication System and User.

The same logic suits for delete action, but TimeSheetOperation value must be changed to "D".

I hope it will help.

Best Regards,

Aliaksandra

entry.title: How to Implement Schema Separation for Multitenancy with PostgreSQL on SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently following the SAP Discovery Center mission "Develop a multitenant SaaS application on SAP BTP using CAP", and I have a question regarding the use of PostgreSQL on SAP BTP (hyperscaler option) as a shared database in a SaaS environment.

The architecture guidelines for data separation in multitenant SaaS applications using CAP recommend the schema separation approach because of its flexibility and cost-effectiveness.

With SAP HANA Cloud, this can be achieved using HDI containers. What technique or recommended approach exists in this case to implement tenant-based schema separation with PostgreSQL on SAP BTP? 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Regulatory Change Manager Tool

Regulatory Change Manager Tool helps with providing timely information on upcoming regulatory changes that can affect SAP products. You can access the tool here.

It is a SaaS application built on SAP Business Technology Platform that provides a centralized repository of global regulatory changes relevant to business processes and SAP products. It serves as a one-stop solution for all regulatory change requirements, offering comprehensive and well-organized information to help organizations rapidly adapt to new and evolving regulations.

Use Cases in Invoicing:

  • Tax Regulation Changes: Monitors changes in tax requirements that affect invoice processing and calculation methods
  • Invoice Format Requirements: Tracks mandatory e-invoicing format changes and submission requirements across different countries
  • Compliance Reporting: Supports preparation of statutory reports and ensures alignment with evolving regulatory frameworks

For more details, refer What Is the Regulatory Change Manager Tool?

entry.title: How to Product Description in Ship HU Form - Extend the HU form
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a functional requirement to display the product description in the Ship HU form. During our analysis, we identified the following issue:
the product description is not available in the form data source, specifically at the item nodes level.

For this reason, we started an investigation to find a standard or technical solution. However: we did not find any available data source extension for this template, it is not possible to add a custom field, since the only available Business Context is reserved for the Header section: Handling Unit (HANDLINGUNIT).

Therefore, we would appreciate your support in identifying a solution or technical alternative to display the product description in the Ship HU form.

Thank you in advance for your support.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Product Description in Ship HU Form - Extend t...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

You may consider BADI Handling Unit Header Output Extension (/SCWM/EX4_OM_HU_EXTEND_HDR). Please refer blow SAP Help Portal page:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/1263590fb2c34f4baa46fbc6292aaad0.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/3dbc868fbf744d6fbbfa83362f82bf0c.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US

 

Best Regards,

entry.title: mobile services mdk app clipboard text
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are building a custom mdk app (BAS + JS + branded app, and CAP backend) for our clients Zebra devices and it seems they have run into the follwing  problem.

They require the Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty controls that have a barcode input to also support paste from clipboard functionality. This works perfect on Android/iphone phones but it seems on zebra devices longpress does not yeld the paste/clipboard options as on phones.

Is there another way to manage copy/paste text from outside of the app? I understand there i also a @nativescript/clipboard approach. Do you have an example of this posted anywhere. Is this a valid option or is there someother support for this?

 

Best Regards, RaulM
